headlineSummary: OpenAI drew the most negative discussion on limited evidence [E2][E3], while Anthropic and Google Gemini had mildly positive mentions that are in a close tie [E1][E2].

ranking.summary: OpenAI was discussed negatively in comparisons that said ChatGPT pales beside Google’s AI efforts and in a benchmark critique of GPT-5.1’s weak task performance, with limited evidence [E2][E3].
Anthropic got a mild positive mention for Claude Opus producing a readable result, with limited evidence and a close tie with Google Gemini [E1].
Google Gemini received a mild positive mention in a broader favorable comparison of Google’s AI stack, and it is in a close tie with Anthropic [E2].

evidence.excerpt: Claude Opus came up with this script: https://pastebin.com/ntE50PkZ It produces a somewhat-readable PDF (first page at least) with this text output:

annotations.rationale: The commenter credits Claude Opus with generating a script that produced a readable result, which is mild praise of model capability.

evidence.excerpt: Google/Alphabet are so vertically integrated for AI when you think about it. Compare what they're doing - their own power generation , their own silicon, their own data centers, search Gmail YouTube Gemini workspace wallet, billions and billions of Android and Chromebook users, their ads everywhere, their browser everywhere, waymo, probably buy back Boston dynamics soon enough (they're recently partnered together), fusion research, drugs discovery.... and then look at ChatGPT's chatbot or grok's porn. Pales in comparison.

annotations.rationale: Gemini is named as part of Google's AI stack in a favorable comparison that portrays Google's AI position as much stronger.
The commenter contrasts Google's AI efforts with ChatGPT and says ChatGPT 'Pales in comparison,' which is mild criticism.

evidence.excerpt: > Without any context provided, the state-of-the-art model, GPT-5.1 (High), is only able to solve less than 1% of tasks. This starkly demonstrates that the data is contamination-free, as the model is almost entirely incapable of solving the tasks without learning from the context. [...] [With context provided,] on average, models solve only 17.2% of tasks. Even the best-performing model, GPT-5.1 (High), achieves just 23.7%.

annotations.rationale: The quoted benchmark result presents GPT-5.1 as performing poorly on the task, using clearly unfavorable performance language like 'only' and 'just'.
